debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
inst✝ : Mul α
a x y : @MulOpposite α
⊢ @SemiconjBy α inst✝ (@MulOpposite.unop α a) (@MulOpposite.unop α y) (@MulOpposite.unop α x) ↔
    @SemiconjBy (@MulOpposite α) (@MulOpposite.instMul α inst✝) a x y

failed_attempts:
by
  simp [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, unop_inj]
  <;>
  (try cases a <;> cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, unop_inj]) <;>
  (try ring_nf) <;>
  (try simp_all [mul_assoc]) <;>
  (try aesop)
  <;>
  (try
    {
      constructor <;>
      intro h <;>
      simp_all [mul_assoc] <;>
      (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try aesop)
    })
EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:

by
  constructor
  · intro h
    -- Prove the forward direction: if SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x), then SemiconjBy a x y
    have h₁ : (unop a : α) * (unop y : α) = (unop x : α) * (unop a : α) := by
      simpa [SemiconjBy] using h
    have h₂ : (a : αᵐᵒᵖ) * (x : αᵐᵒᵖ) = (y : αᵐᵒᵖ) * (a : αᵐᵒᵖ) := by
      -- Use the definition of multiplication in αᵐᵒᵖ to transform h₁ into h₂
      simp [MulOpposite.mul] at h₁ ⊢
      <;>
      (try simp_all [SemiconjBy]) <;>
      (try simp_all [MulOpposite.unop_inj]) <;>
      (try simp_all [mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]) <;>

      (try simp_all [Univ.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fintype.card_fin]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one, Nat.cast_zero]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.castSucc_zero, Fin.castSucc_succ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ])
      <;>
      simp_all [unop_inj]
      <;>

      try linarith

          <;>

    -- Use h₂ to conclude the proof
    simpa [SemiconjBy] using h₂
  · intro h
    -- Prove the reverse direction: if SemiconjBy a x y, then SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x)

      simpa [SemiconjBy] using h
    have h₂ : (unop a : α) * (unop y : α) = (unop x : α) * (unop a : α) := by
      -- Use the definition of multiplication in αᵐᵒᵖ to transform h₁ into h₂
      simp [MulOpposite.mul] at h₁ ⊢
      <;>

      (try simp_all [mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm]) <;>

by
  have h_main : (unop a : α) * unop y = unop x * unop a ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if (unop a) * unop y = unop x * unop a, then a * x = y * a
      intro h
      -- Extract the underlying elements of a, x, y as a', x', y'
      have h₁ : a.val * x.val = y.val * a.val := by
        -- Simplify the equation using the definition of multiplication in MulOpposite
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_coe, ← mul_assoc] at h ⊢
        <;>
        (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try ring_nf at h ⊢ <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try nlinarith) <;>
        (try linarith)
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            ring_nf at h ⊢ <;>
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            linarith
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            nlinarith
          })
      -- Use the underlying elements to prove the desired equality in MulOpposite
      cases a <;> cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mk.injEq]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try nlinarith) <;>
      (try linarith)
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if a * x = y * a, then (unop a) * unop y = unop x * unop a
      intro h
      -- Extract the underlying elements of a, x, y as a', x', y'
      have h₁ : (unop a : α) * unop y = unop x * unop a := by
        -- Simplify the equation using the definition of multiplication in MulOpposite
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_coe, ← mul_assoc] at h ⊢
        <;>
        (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try ring_nf at h ⊢ <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
        (try nlinarith) <;>
        (try linarith)
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            ring_nf at h ⊢ <;>
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            linarith
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]
            <;>
            nlinarith
          })
      -- Use the underlying elements to prove the desired equality in MulOpposite
      cases a <;> cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mk.injEq]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try nlinarith) <;>
      (try linarith)
  -- Use the main lemma to complete the proof
  simp only [SemiconjBy, h_main]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
  (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_coe, mul_assoc]) <;>
  (try nlinarith) <;>
  (try linarith)
  <;>

      <;>

    <;> try simp_all [comp]
    <;> try ring_nf at *

by
  have h1 : @SemiconjBy α _ (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) ↔ (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := rfl
  have h2 : @SemiconjBy αᵐᵒᵖ _ a x y ↔ a * x = y * a := rfl
  have h3 : (unop a : α) * (unop y : α) = (unop x : α) * (unop a : α) ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [unop_inj]) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try simp_all [unop_inj]) <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>

        intro h <;>
        simp_all [unop_inj] <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [unop_inj]) <;>
        (try aesop)
      }
    )
  simp_all [h1, h2]
  <;>

by
  simp [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, mul_assoc]

  <;>

  <;> tauto

by
  have h_main : (unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a) ↔ (a * x = y * a) := by
    constructor
    · intro h
      -- Assuming unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a, we need to prove a * x = y * a.
      -- Since the multiplication in αᵐᵒᵖ is defined as (x * y)ᵐᵒᵖ = (y * x) in α, we can use this property.
      have h₁ : unop (a * x) = unop x * unop a := by
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      have h₂ : unop (y * a) = unop a * unop y := by
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      -- Using the given equality, we can substitute and simplify.
      have h₃ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := by
        rw [h₁, h₂]
        <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
        <;> try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
        <;> try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      -- Since unop is injective, we can conclude a * x = y * a.
      have h₄ : a * x = y * a := by
        apply MulOpposite.unop_inj.mp
        exact h₃
      exact h₄
    · intro h
      -- Assuming a * x = y * a, we need to prove unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a.
      -- Using the property of αᵐᵒᵖ multiplication, we can derive the required equality.
      have h₁ : unop (a * x) = unop x * unop a := by
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      have h₂ : unop (y * a) = unop a * unop y := by
        simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      -- Since a * x = y * a, we have unop (a * x) = unop (y * a).
      have h₃ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := by
        rw [h]
      -- Substituting the expressions for unop (a * x) and unop (y * a), we get the desired result.
      have h₄ : unop x * unop a = unop a * unop y := by
        calc
          unop x * unop a = unop (a * x) := by rw [h₁]
          _ = unop (y * a) := by rw [h₃]
          _ = unop a * unop y := by rw [h₂]
      -- Using the commutativity of equality, we can conclude the proof.
      have h₅ : unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a := by
        calc
          unop a * unop y = unop a * unop y := rfl
          _ = unop x * unop a := by
            rw [← h₄]
            <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def]
      exact h₅
  
  -- Using the established equivalence, we can now prove the main statement.
  simp_all [SemiconjBy]
  <;>
  aesop

by
  have h_main : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a), then a * x = y * a
      intro h
      have h₁ : a * x = y * a := by
        cases a; cases x; cases y

        <;>
        nlinarith
      exact h₁
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if a * x = y * a, then (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a)
      intro h
      have h₁ : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
        cases a; cases x; cases y
        simp_all [MulOpposite.mk.injEq, MulOpposite.mul_def]
        <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;>
        try simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]) <;>
        (try nlinarith) <;>
        (try
          {
            congr 1 <;>
            simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] <;>
            nlinarith
          })
        <;>
        simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
        <;>
        nlinarith
      exact h₁
  
  simp only [SemiconjBy] at *
  exact h_main

      simp_all [Prod.mk.injEq]
      <;>
      tauto
    })

by
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) → SemiconjBy a x y
    intro h
    -- Simplify the hypothesis and the goal using the definition of SemiconjBy and MulOpposite's multiplication
    simp [SemiconjBy, Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff] at h ⊢
    <;> simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at * <;> aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try aesop)
  · -- Prove the backward direction: SemiconjBy a x y → SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x)
    intro h
    -- Simplify the hypothesis and the goal using the definition of SemiconjBy and MulOpposite's multiplication
    simp [SemiconjBy, Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff] at h ⊢
    <;> simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at * <;> aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Mul.mul, Prod.ext_iff]) <;>
    (try aesop)

simp [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, unop_inj]
  <;>
  (try aesop)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [MulOpposite.mul, unop_inj])
  <;>
  (try ring_nf at *)
  <;>
  (try tauto)

def SemiconjBy [Mul M] (a x y : M) : Prop :=
  a * x = y * a

structure MulOpposite (α : Type u) : Type u where
  op :: unop : α

postfix:max "ᵐᵒᵖ" := MulOpposite

namespace MulOpposite

instance [Mul α] : Mul αᵐᵒᵖ where mul x y := op (unop y * unop x)

@[simp] theorem unop_inj {x y : αᵐᵒᵖ} : unop x = unop y ↔ x = y := by
  cases x; cases y; simp

#guard_msgs (drop warning) in
@[simp] theorem semiconj_by_unop [Mul α] {a x y : αᵐᵒᵖ} :
    SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) ↔ SemiconjBy a x y := 
  simp [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, unop_inj, mul_comm]
  <;>
  aesop
